retrofit_needed.hero_note: Unsplash rate limit exceeded at build. Hero reused from sibling page (master-0106, Ricky Beron @nacio1966, New Orleans river view). Retrofit with a separate Louisiana Baton Rouge State Capitol or French Quarter photo when rate limit resets.
retrofit_needed.mi_notes: No Meeting Intelligence search run in sprint mode. Pullquote uses Tom Price-Daniel brand voice (exempt from MI provenance). Retrofit with Joanna/Paulina/Antony MI quote if Louisiana termination or civil-code discussion surfaces.
retrofit_needed.neighbour_notes: Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Maine sibling state pages exist but Louisiana-specific state pages (parent + other topic children) not yet built. Add cross-state links when wave-4 ships.

sameness_check.opening_anchor_1: La. Civ. Code art. 2747 — only US state where at-will derives from civil code
sameness_check.opening_anchor_2: R.S. 23:631 sooner-of-15-days rule (inverse of Kentucky later-of framework)
sameness_check.tagline_anchor_1: La. Civ. Code art. 2749 fixed-term contract trap (offer letter with project duration)
sameness_check.tagline_anchor_2: R.S. 23:631 sooner-of-15-days vs Kentucky later-of-14-days cross-batch contrast
sameness_check.tagline_anchor_3: R.S. 23:632 90-day penalty as steepest in the batch
sameness_check.notes: All three opening anchors and all three tagline anchors are Louisiana-specific facts not appearing in Iowa/Kansas/Kentucky/Maine pages. Civil-code origin is unique to Louisiana among all 50 US states.
